title: Crispy Ethiopian Lentils Stew
description: A hearty, one‑pot lentil stew with a tangy tamarind ball and a crisp top from broiling, perfect for a quick, Jain‑friendly lunch.

ingredients.name: tamarind ball
ingredients.notes: pre‑soaked in 1 cup warm water for 20 min, drained

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, grated ginger, and berbere, cumin, and coriander; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add rinsed lentils and stir to coat with spices.
Pour in 4 cups of water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until lentils are tender, about 25 minutes.
While the lentils cook, mash the pre‑soaked tamarind ball with a fork until it forms a smooth paste.
Stir the tamarind paste into the pot, then add lemon juice and salt; adjust seasoning if needed.
Transfer the stew to a shallow baking dish, spread it evenly, and broil on high for 3–5 minutes until a crisp, golden crust forms.
Remove from the oven, let rest for a minute, then serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh herbs.
Enjoy your crispy, tangy Ethiopian lentil stew!
